label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which of the statements is true based on the passage provided. The passage starts by talking about the various uses of computers, including solving problems, controlling machines, calculators for simple math, large computers for scientists handling many problems at once, gaming, language learning, and the Kurzweil Reading Machine for blind people. Now, looking at the options: A) Computers can solve all the problems people can't do. Hmm, the passage says computers can solve difficult problems, but "all the problems people can't do" sounds too absolute. The text mentions they can solve many and difficult ones, but not necessarily all. So probably A is incorrect. B) Large computers can only solve one problem at a time. The passage says "Scientists use large computers to solve many problems at the same time." So B says they can only solve one, which is the opposite of what's stated. So B is false. C) When using the Kurzweil machine, a blind man needn't do anything. From the text: "To use the Kurzweil machine, a blind person can simply open the book and put it down on the scanner. The machine 'reads' the words... When one page is finished, the blind person turns the page and puts the book on the scanner again." So the user does need to put the book on the scanner and turn pages. So C is false because the blind person does have to do some actions. D) The Kurzweil machine can help blind people and short-sighted children. The passage states: "Blind people like the Kurzweil machine... Many libraries now have Kurzweil Machine for blind people
